quizzes.title: Basic Vocabulary Quiz
quizzes.description: Test your knowledge of basic English words

questions.question: What is the opposite of 'hot'?
questions.explanation: Cold is the direct opposite of hot.

questions.question: Which word means 'not expensive'?
questions.explanation: Cheap means not expensive or low in price.

questions.question: I __ coffee every morning.
questions.explanation: Use present simple 'drink' for habitual actions.

questions.question: 'Beautiful' means ugly.
questions.explanation: False. Beautiful means attractive and pleasant to look at.

questions.question: What is a 'teacher'?
options: A person who teaches in a school
questions.explanation: A teacher is a person who teaches in a school.

quizzes.title: Past Tense Grammar Quiz
quizzes.description: Test your understanding of past tense usage

questions.question: Complete: I __ to the store yesterday.
questions.explanation: Use past simple 'went' for completed actions.

questions.question: How long __ you __ English?
questions.explanation: Use present perfect for duration from past to now.

questions.question: She __ reading a book when I arrived.
questions.explanation: Use 'was' with past continuous for 'she'.

questions.question: 'I have eaten' is present perfect tense.
questions.explanation: True. 'Have eaten' is present perfect tense.

questions.question: Complete: If it __ tomorrow, we will stay home.
questions.explanation: In first conditional, use present simple in the 'if' clause.

quizzes.title: Advanced Vocabulary Quiz
quizzes.description: Test advanced vocabulary and word usage

questions.question: What does 'ambiguous' mean?
options: Open to more than one interpretation
questions.explanation: Ambiguous means something is unclear or has multiple interpretations.

questions.question: Which word means 'lasting for a very short time'?
questions.explanation: Ephemeral means lasting for a very short time.

questions.question: The architect was __ in planning every detail.
questions.explanation: Meticulous means showing great attention to detail.

questions.question: 'Pragmatic' means idealistic and theoretical.
questions.explanation: False. Pragmatic means practical and realistic, not idealistic.

questions.question: What is a 'counterargument'?
questions.explanation: A counterargument is an opposing viewpoint in a debate.

quizzes.title: Advanced Grammar and Expression
quizzes.description: Test complex grammar structures and sophisticated expression

questions.question: Which sentence is correctly formed with inversion for emphasis?
options: Never I have seen such beauty.
Never have I seen such beauty.
Never I seen have such beauty.
Never have seen I such beauty.
questions.explanation: Correct inversion places the auxiliary before the subject.

questions.question: The politician attempted to __ the real issues in the debate.
questions.explanation: Obfuscate means to make unclear or difficult to understand.

questions.question: 'Perspicacious' means showing keen insight and understanding.
questions.explanation: True. Perspicacious means having keen insight and understanding.

quizzes.title: IELTS Level 5 Quiz
quizzes.description: Practice for IELTS 5 level English proficiency

questions.question: What does 'deteriorate' mean?
questions.explanation: Deteriorate means to become progressively worse.

questions.question: The new policies will help to __ the negative effects of inflation.
questions.explanation: Mitigate means to make less severe or serious.
